The UAE has provided more than $3 billion in aid to Gaza since 2023, delivering over 90,000 tons via 8,700 trucks, 80 airdrops and 13 ships, with UAE-supported facilities providing medical care to 75,000 Gazans.
The UAE announced a $30 million Sudan emergency response and pledged $500 million to a Sudan Humanitarian Fund in February 2026, amid confirmed famine in Sudan.
The UAE sent 30 tonnes of food aid to Kenya in February 2026 as direct bilateral food assistance in response to deepening La Niña-driven drought and food insecurity.
The UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs publicly condemned the targeting of a WFP humanitarian convoy in Sudan and the UAE publicly supports WFP humanitarian operations in Sudan.

The UAE and EU held a DG ECHO co-hosted panel on global humanitarian supply-chain resilience in Brussels on 11 June 2026 with over 80 senior representatives, and maintain a working humanitarian-logistics dialogue.
The UAE and Qatar both co-signed the joint ministerial statement advancing a maritime corridor to deliver humanitarian assistance to Gaza, alongside the UK, US, Cyprus and the European Commission.
A 2024 academic study in The International Spectator comparatively analyses Qatar and the UAE as rival Gulf donors in Sudan.
The UAE, EU, Qatar and Kenya operate a joint co-chair mechanism on Sudan de-escalation, having co-chaired the ministerial meeting on 24 September 2025.
The Tony Elumelu Foundation partnered with the UAE Office of Development Affairs and the Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation to empower an additional 1,000 young African entrepreneurs.

ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD, leads the UAE $1 billion AI for Development initiative targeting digital infrastructure across Africa, linking UAE development finance to African digital infrastructure and announced in a G20 context.

The UAE contributed $1.5 million to an ADB fund promoting agricultural innovation in May 2026, made via the International Affairs Office at the UAE Presidential Court in support of Asia-Pacific food security.
AD Ports Group and Masdar entered a partnership in November 2025 for global offshore wind logistics.
The UAE–India CEPA, signed February 2022, drove bilateral trade to USD 100 billion in FY2024-25 with a USD 200 billion target by 2032, serving as the template for CEPA impact benchmarking.

Under Operation Birds of Goodness the UAE carried out its 69th humanitarian airdrop over Gaza, with airdrops cumulatively delivering more than 3,924 tonnes of aid; convoy operations under Operation Chivalrous Knight 3 delivered 416 tonnes via 47 trucks in July 2026 and passed the 300-convoy milestone.

Theyab bin Mohamed bin Zayed, appointed chairman of the International Humanitarian and Philanthropic Council under Federal Decree No. 94 of 2026, attended the International Development Dialogue hosted by the Office of Development Affairs.

UN OCHA independently tracks UAE humanitarian funding through its Financial Tracking Service, recording $94,336,382 in funded and $133,015,246 in paid UAE humanitarian flows for 2026, with the UAE tracked as donor #100.
The UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs condemned the targeting of a WFP humanitarian convoy in Sudan, publicly backing WFP operations there.
A 2024 academic study in The International Spectator comparatively analyses Qatar and the UAE as rival Gulf donors competing in aid engagement in Sudan.
The UAE and the United Kingdom co-signed the joint ministerial statement on advancing a maritime corridor to deliver humanitarian assistance to Gaza.
The UAE and the United States co-signed the joint ministerial statement on the Gaza humanitarian maritime corridor.
The UAE and Cyprus jointly backed the ministerial coalition advancing a maritime corridor to deliver humanitarian assistance to Gaza.
The UAE and the European Union co-chaired the 24 September 2025 ministerial meeting coordinating international Sudan de-escalation efforts within a joint co-chair mechanism.
Dubai Humanitarian participated in the UAE–EU humanitarian supply-chain resilience panel co-hosted by DG ECHO in Brussels on 11 June 2026, attended by 80+ senior representatives.

ADEX, the export-financing arm of the Abu Dhabi Fund for Development, leads the UAE's $1 billion AI for Development initiative linking UAE development finance to digital infrastructure across Africa, announced in a G20 context.
The UAE foreign-aid agency co-leads the $1 billion AI for Development initiative with ADEX, the export-financing arm of ADFD.
The UAE contributed $1.5 million via the International Affairs Office at the UAE Presidential Court to an ADB fund promoting agricultural innovation and food security in Asia-Pacific in May 2026.
The Tony Elumelu Foundation partnered with the UAE Office of Development Affairs and the Khalifa Bin Zayed Al Nahyan Foundation to empower an additional 1,000 young African entrepreneurs.
The Office of Development Affairs at the UAE Presidential Court held a joint event with the UAE Embassy in Beijing in July 2026.

The UAE President established the UAE Aid Agency by Federal Decree No. 27 of 2024, its founding instrument, as announced by the UAE National Media Authority; the agency operates an open-data transparency portal and published the UAE Foreign Aid Report 2024.
UN OCHA's Financial Tracking Service independently tracks the UAE as donor #100, recording $94,336,382 in funded and $133,015,246 in paid UAE humanitarian flows for 2026.
Independent analysis published by AllAfrica in May 2026 documents the expanding rise of Emirati aid engagement across Africa.
